name: atlas
concept: On-chain AI canon-keeper for a shared fictional world: explorers claim a region of a persistent map and describe its lore, and an injection-resistant AI cartographer rules each claim CANON / CONTESTED / APOCRYPHA with a 0-100 coherence score against established world history under validator consensus, growing a single tamper-evident world atlas on-chain.
appArchetype: persistent world-map view (you land on a living shared map and navigate region to region; the cartographic canvas is the primary surface with claims plotted on it, not a landing page or a feed)

palette.notes: Aged parchment vellum, sepia-ink linework, a verdant cartographer's-green accent with oxblood region borders, compass roses and contour hatching, illuminated drop-capitals, zero flat digital fills or glassy panels.

heroMotif: an animated hand-drawn parchment map whose coastlines and contour hatching ink themselves in, with a slowly rotating compass rose and drifting sea stipple
layoutMotif: cartographic canvas: a pannable parchment world map as the main surface with plotted region claims, a slim gazetteer side-margin of regions, and an illuminated claim cartouche that opens over the map; no hero, no stat strip
readmeShape: explorer's gazetteer (a numbered regional field guide with map legends and an entry per public method)
description: Atlas is an on-chain AI canon-keeper for a shared fictional world drawn on a 12x12 grid, built on GenLayer, where the cartographer's ruling is the settlement itself. An explorer claims a region coordinate and writes its lore, and an injection-resistant AI cartographer rules the claim CANON, CONTESTED, or APOCRYPHA with a 0-100 coherence score against the established canon of neighbouring regions. A leader proposes the ruling and every validator re-runs it, agreeing on the ruling exactly and the coherence within tolerance. Deterministic guards check the coordinate, name, and lore and reject claimed tiles before the model, and a backstop clamps the score into its ruling band after. Only a CANON ruling occupies a tile and grows the atlas, while a static frontend stages the consensus.
